---
title: "Prototype Roadmap"
chapter_id: "prototype-roadmap"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "viea"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "scf"
  - "vcm_public"
  - "planforge"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "beastbrain_timeless"
  - "bugbrain"
  - "moecot"
  - "moecot_md"
  - "road_to_agi"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "project_theseus_whitepaper"
  - "theseus_plan_compiler"
  - "theseus_self_evolution_system"
  - "theseus_architecture_gate"
  - "theseus_operator_os"
  - "theseus_circle_transfer"
  - "circle_ai_contract_suite"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Prototype Roadmap

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `prototype-roadmap` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `viea`, `benchmaxxing`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `bugbrain`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `road_to_agi`, `coherence_exchange`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `theseus_plan_compiler`, `theseus_self_evolution_system`, `theseus_architecture_gate`, `theseus_operator_os`, `theseus_circle_transfer`, `circle_ai_contract_suite` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs a build sequence that does not introduce agency or self-improvement before auditability and governance are credible.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A roadmap that jumps to autonomous improvement skips source matrices, artifact graphs, claim ledgers, tests, and authority controls.

## Core Claim

[prototype-roadmap.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] The prototype should grow in staged increments from source matrix and artifact graph to governed capability replacement.

## Mechanism

- Start with source inventory, claim ledger, artifact graph, and validation.
- Add planner, VCM, typed execution, verification, benchmark ratchets, and SCF gates in order.
- Delay recursive self-improvement until evaluator and governance integrity are credible.

## Interfaces

- Each phase unlocks a later layer.
- Evidence gates decide promotion.
- Changelog and releases document progress.

## Invariants

- Phases have acceptance criteria.
- Self-improvement is gated.
- Roadmap milestones are not test results.

## Failure modes

- Building agency before auditability.
- Skipping verification because prototypes feel useful.
- Treating roadmap as evidence.

## Minimal implementation

A phase table with deliverables, gates, blockers, and acceptance criteria.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Phase acceptance checklist | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Dependency gate review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Prototype evidence-state audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. |
| `planforge` | PlanForge | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Planning substrate. Goal-to-execution compilation, hierarchical decomposition, DAG planning, scheduling, intelligence arbitrage. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `beastbrain_timeless` | BeastBrain Architecture: Timeless Edition | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Definitive standalone master plan variant. |
| `bugbrain` | BugBrain | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Edge/resource-aware implementation context. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `moecot_md` | moecot_agent_whitepaper.md | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Markdown export/source version. |
| `road_to_agi` | Road To AGI | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Remaining work / roadmap context. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `project_theseus_whitepaper` | Project Theseus Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Local-first report-driven RMI implementation reference: SymLiquid, SparkStream, Octopus Router, residual escrow, self-evolution gates, Hive runtime, observability. |
| `theseus_plan_compiler` | Theseus Plan Compiler | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Goal-to-contract compiler with semantic IR DAGs, VCM context slices, executor routes, claim/evidence targets, contract hashes, and replay traces. |
| `theseus_self_evolution_system` | Theseus Self-Evolution System | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Evidence-first self-evolution lane with intervention ladder, ATTD repo-health gate, guarded teacher self-edit, architecture experiment governance, loop closure, and outcome ledger. |
| `theseus_architecture_gate` | Theseus Architecture Gate | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Pre-training readiness gate covering ratchet completeness, router readiness, safety ledger, residual escrow, bridge benchmarks, procedural tools, routing memory, lifecycle governance, and external-inference zero. |
| `theseus_operator_os` | Hive Operator OS and Work Board | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Shared command vocabulary, durable SQLite work board, node registry, background/watch/wake contracts, skill registry, tool hooks, feedback routing, and safety-visible operator surface. |
| `theseus_circle_transfer` | Theseus Circle Calculus Transfer Lane | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Report-only bridge from Circle finite fixtures into private Theseus benchmark design with explicit quality/runtime/memory/transfer/failure-case claim boundaries. |
| `circle_ai_contract_suite` | Circle Calculus AI Contract Suite | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Theorem-linked AI contract families for RoPE, KV-cache freshness, sparse attention, recurrence schedules, strided fanout, cyclic memory, multicoil phase, cyclic mixers, and seed-rule regeneration. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
